name: Hex Data Notebook API
what_it_does: Collaborative data notebook platform combining SQL and Python cells in a shareable workspace — with API for running projects programmatically, enabling teams to build interactive data apps, dashboards, and analytical workflows that non-technical stakeholders can use.
use_cases: Running Hex projects via API to generate fresh reports on a schedule
Triggering data analysis workflows from external systems via project run API
Embedding Hex-generated outputs in other applications via run results API
Orchestrating parameterized data analysis runs with different input values
Building data app automation pipelines that refresh dashboards on demand
not_for: Real-time streaming analytics (use Flink or Spark Streaming)
Production ML model serving (use MLflow or SageMaker)
Raw data engineering pipelines without analytics layer (use dbt or Airbyte)
best_when: A data team needs collaborative SQL+Python notebooks with shareable apps and wants to run projects programmatically via API for automation or scheduled refreshes.
avoid_when: You need self-hosted notebooks, real-time streaming, or a free-forever collaborative notebook platform.

auth.notes: API token from workspace settings. Bearer token in Authorization header. Workspace-scoped tokens. No fine-grained scopes — token has access to all projects in workspace. Separate tokens per workspace.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 1 user, unlimited projects, 10 published apps
pricing.notes: API access available on Team and Enterprise plans — not free tier. Free tier is single-user. Strong focus on collaboration and sharing data apps with non-technical users. US-based, well-funded (Series B). API access for automation requires paid plan.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API covers project runs, run status, and run results. Async run model — create run then poll status. Webhook support for run completion. Limited to run management — cannot create/edit projects via API.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Each run creates a new run record — not idempotent. Run status reads are idempotent. Input parameter runs create distinct run instances.
known_agent_gotchas: API access requires Team or Enterprise plan — not available on free tier
Project runs are async — must poll run status until complete or use webhook
Cannot create or edit projects via API — only run existing projects
Run inputs must match project input schema — validation errors not detailed

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR compliant. Workspace-scoped tokens with no fine-grained scopes. Secrets managed via workspace secret store (not in notebooks). US data residency. SSO via SAML/OIDC on Enterprise.
